title: Landing page color system + typography tokens
description: Define a separate design token set for the landing/marketing page distinct from the app. Use #0A0A0F as the primary background, Inter Display for headings, and Inter for body text. Create CSS custom properties and Tailwind theme extensions scoped to the landing page layout.
acceptanceCriteria: CSS custom properties defined for landing page palette (#0A0A0F bg, accent gradients, text hierarchy)
Tailwind config extended with `landing` prefix tokens for colors and font families
Inter Display loaded via next/font with display swap and applied to all landing headings

title: Navbar with sticky frosted glass effect and mobile hamburger
description: Build the landing page navbar with sticky positioning, a frosted glass backdrop-blur effect on scroll, smooth scroll detection to toggle compact mode, and a mobile hamburger menu with slide-in drawer.
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar sticks to top with backdrop-blur and bg opacity transition triggered by scroll position > 50px
Mobile hamburger button visible below 768px that opens a full-height slide-in drawer with nav links
All nav links smooth-scroll to corresponding section IDs on the page

title: Hero section with gradient headline and dual CTAs
description: Create the hero section with a large gradient-filled headline, subtitle, two CTA buttons (primary: 'Start Building Free', secondary: 'Watch Demo'), and an animated gradient mesh background.
acceptanceCriteria: Headline uses a CSS gradient text fill (background-clip: text) with the brand color ramp
Two CTA buttons rendered with distinct styles: primary filled gradient, secondary outlined with hover fill
Animated gradient mesh background renders behind the hero content (delegates to LND-016 for WebGL)

title: MiniSimulator embedded interactive demo
description: Build an interactive mini URL shortener demo embedded in the landing page. Users type a URL, click 'Shorten', and see an animated particle flow visualization showing the request path through Load Balancer -> App Server -> Cache -> Database.
acceptanceCriteria: Input field accepts a URL and a 'Shorten' button triggers the animation sequence
Particle flow animates through 4 labeled nodes (LB, App, Cache, DB) with colored particles
Demo is self-contained with no backend calls; all logic runs client-side with mock data

title: Trust Bar with auto-scrolling company logos
description: Create a trust bar section displaying grayscale company logos in an infinite auto-scrolling horizontal carousel. Logos pause on hover and resume on mouse leave.
acceptanceCriteria: Logos render in grayscale via CSS filter and transition to color on hover
Infinite scroll animation uses CSS translateX keyframes with duplicated logo set for seamless loop
Animation pauses on hover and respects prefers-reduced-motion media query

title: Product Showcase with 3 tabbed sections
description: Build a tabbed product showcase section with three tabs: System Design, Algorithm Visualization, and Interview Prep. Each tab shows a screenshot/mockup, feature bullets, and a CTA link.
acceptanceCriteria: Three tabs render with active state indicator and smooth content transition on switch
Each tab panel contains a preview image/mockup, 3-4 feature bullet points, and a 'Learn More' link
Tab state is managed client-side with keyboard arrow key navigation for accessibility

title: Feature Deep-Dive with alternating layout and scroll animations
description: Create 5 feature deep-dive sections with alternating left-right image/text layout. Each section includes an icon, heading, description paragraph, and a feature image. Sections animate in on scroll.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 sections rendered with alternating image-left/text-right and text-left/image-right layouts
Each section triggers a fade-in + slide animation when entering the viewport via IntersectionObserver
Content is data-driven from a features array for easy addition/removal of sections

title: How It Works 3-step horizontal layout
description: Build a 'How It Works' section with 3 steps displayed horizontally: 1) Choose a Problem, 2) Build Your Design, 3) Simulate & Learn. Steps are connected by animated dotted lines.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 steps rendered in a horizontal row on desktop with numbered circles, titles, and descriptions
Connecting dotted lines between steps animate sequentially using CSS animation-delay
Layout stacks vertically on mobile (<768px) with vertical connecting lines

title: Social Proof Wall with masonry testimonial grid
description: Create a masonry-style testimonial grid displaying user quotes with avatar, name, role, and company. Cards have varying heights to create the masonry effect using CSS columns.
acceptanceCriteria: Masonry grid renders using CSS column-count (3 cols desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile)
Each testimonial card shows avatar, name, role/company, star rating, and quote text
At least 9 placeholder testimonials are included with realistic content

title: Comparison Table (Architex vs Traditional prep)
description: Build a comparison table with 7 feature rows comparing Architex against traditional interview prep methods. Use checkmarks, x-marks, and descriptive cells with visual distinction for the Architex column.
acceptanceCriteria: Table renders 7 feature rows with Architex and Traditional columns, plus a feature label column
Architex column is visually highlighted with brand color background and checkmark icons
Table is horizontally scrollable on mobile with sticky first column for feature labels

title: Pricing section with 3 tiers and annual toggle
description: Create the pricing section with 3 plan tiers (Free, Pro, Team), a monthly/annual billing toggle showing 20% savings, feature lists per tier, and a 'Most Popular' badge on the Pro tier.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 pricing cards rendered with plan name, price, feature list, and CTA button for each tier
Annual/monthly toggle switches displayed prices with smooth number transition animation
Pro tier card has a 'Most Popular' badge and elevated visual treatment (border glow or shadow)

title: Final CTA section with gradient background
description: Build the final call-to-action section near the page bottom with a full-width gradient background, urgency-driven headline, subtext, and a prominent CTA button.
acceptanceCriteria: Section spans full width with a brand gradient background (matching hero palette)
Headline conveys urgency (e.g., 'Start Acing Your System Design Interviews Today')
CTA button is large, high-contrast, and links to the signup/app entry point

title: Footer with 4-column links, newsletter, and social icons
description: Create the landing page footer with 4 columns of categorized links (Product, Resources, Company, Legal), a newsletter email signup input, and social media icon links.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 link columns render with appropriate headings and at least 4 links each
Newsletter signup input with email validation and submit button included in footer
Social media icons (Twitter/X, GitHub, LinkedIn, Discord) link to placeholder URLs

title: Mobile responsive layout for entire landing page
description: Ensure all landing page sections are fully responsive below 768px: single-column layouts, stacked elements, hamburger navigation, appropriately sized typography, and touch-friendly tap targets.
acceptanceCriteria: All sections render in single-column layout below 768px with no horizontal overflow
Typography scales down appropriately (headings, body text) using clamp() or responsive utilities
All interactive elements (buttons, links, tabs) have minimum 44px tap targets on mobile

title: SEO metadata for landing page
description: Add complete SEO metadata to the landing page including title, description, Open Graph tags, Twitter Card tags, canonical URL, and JSON-LD structured data for a SoftwareApplication.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js metadata export includes title, description, canonical URL, and robots directives
Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags set with title, description, image URL, and site name
JSON-LD structured data for SoftwareApplication type embedded in the page head

title: WebGL gradient mesh background with CSS fallback
description: Create a lightweight WebGL animated gradient mesh for the hero background. Must be under 10KB of JS, maintain 60fps, and gracefully fall back to a CSS radial-gradient animation when WebGL is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: WebGL shader renders an animated gradient mesh with smooth color transitions at 60fps
Total JS bundle for the WebGL component is under 10KB gzipped
CSS fallback with animated radial-gradient activates when WebGL context creation fails

title: Animated text effects (gradient text, typewriter code snippets)
description: Build reusable animated text components: a gradient text component with animated color cycling, and a typewriter effect that types out code snippets character-by-character with a blinking cursor.
acceptanceCriteria: GradientText component animates background-position to cycle gradient colors across text
TypewriterCode component types out a configurable code string with blinking cursor and configurable speed
Both components respect prefers-reduced-motion by disabling animations when set to reduce

title: Scroll-triggered animations for landing page sections
description: Implement a reusable scroll-triggered animation system using IntersectionObserver. Sections fade in and slide up when they enter the viewport. Provide a useScrollReveal hook and a ScrollReveal wrapper component.
acceptanceCriteria: useScrollReveal hook uses IntersectionObserver with configurable threshold and rootMargin
ScrollReveal wrapper component applies fade+slide CSS transitions when element becomes visible
Animations only trigger once per element and respect prefers-reduced-motion

title: Demo video script and recording
description: Write a 90-second product walkthrough video script covering: intro hook, problem statement, live demo of building a system design, simulation running, and closing CTA. Record and edit the video.
acceptanceCriteria: Written script covers 5 beats: hook (10s), problem (15s), demo build (30s), simulation (20s), CTA (15s)
Video recorded at 1080p with clear narration and screen recording of the product
Final video exported as MP4 under 15MB and uploaded to a CDN-friendly location

title: A/B test framework for CTA copy
description: Implement a lightweight A/B testing framework that randomly assigns visitors to test variants for CTA button copy. Track variant assignment in localStorage and fire analytics events for conversion tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: ABTest component accepts variants array and renders one randomly, persisting assignment in localStorage
Analytics event fires on CTA click with variant ID for conversion measurement
Test configuration is data-driven and supports multiple concurrent experiments

title: 75 HLD problem SEO pages with generateStaticParams
description: Create SEO-optimized static pages for all 75 high-level design problems using Next.js generateStaticParams. Each page uses a shared template with problem-specific metadata, requirements summary, and internal links.
acceptanceCriteria: generateStaticParams returns slugs for all 75 HLD problems from the content data source
Each page renders with unique title, meta description, and canonical URL
Pages score 90+ on Lighthouse SEO audit with no missing meta tags

title: 65 LLD problem SEO pages
description: Create SEO-optimized static pages for all 65 low-level design problems using the same generateStaticParams pattern as HLD. Each page uses a shared template with LLD-specific content.
acceptanceCriteria: generateStaticParams returns slugs for all 65 LLD problems
Each page renders with unique title, meta description, canonical URL, and structured data
All pages include breadcrumb navigation and related problem links

title: 50 design pattern SEO pages
description: Create SEO-optimized static pages for 50 design patterns (e.g., CQRS, Event Sourcing, Circuit Breaker). Each page includes pattern description, use cases, diagram preview, and related patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: generateStaticParams returns slugs for all 50 design patterns
Each page includes pattern name, description, when-to-use section, and diagram preview image
Internal links connect related patterns bidirectionally

title: 80 concept SEO pages
description: Create SEO-optimized static pages for 80 system design concepts (e.g., CAP Theorem, Consistent Hashing, Load Balancing). Each page includes explanation, visualization preview, and interview questions.
acceptanceCriteria: generateStaticParams returns slugs for all 80 concepts
Each page includes concept explanation, interactive visualization preview, and 3-5 interview questions
Pages link to related problems and patterns that use the concept

title: Problem page template (requirements, diagram, FAQ)
description: Build the shared problem page template used by HLD and LLD SEO pages. Includes sections for functional/non-functional requirements, architecture diagram preview, key concepts list, capacity estimation summary, and an FAQ accordion.
acceptanceCriteria: Template renders requirements, diagram preview, key concepts, estimation, and FAQ sections from props
FAQ section uses an accessible accordion with proper ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation
Template includes JSON-LD structured data (FAQPage schema) generated from FAQ content

title: Concept page template (visualization, explanation, interview Qs)
description: Build the shared concept page template used by concept and pattern SEO pages. Includes an interactive visualization embed, detailed explanation section, and curated interview questions.
acceptanceCriteria: Template renders visualization placeholder, explanation with markdown support, and interview questions
Visualization section lazy-loads the interactive component with a static preview fallback
Interview questions section includes expandable answer hints

title: JSON-LD structured data (Course + LearningResource)
description: Implement JSON-LD structured data generation for Course and LearningResource schema types. Inject into page heads for all SEO pages to improve search result rich snippets.
acceptanceCriteria: Course schema generated for the main learning tracks with provider, description, and offers
LearningResource schema generated per problem/concept page with educationalLevel and learningResourceType
JSON-LD validates cleanly against Google's Rich Results Test with no errors

title: Dynamic OG image generation (/api/og/[type]/[slug])
description: Build an API route using @vercel/og (Satori) to dynamically generate Open Graph images per page type and slug. Images include the page title, type badge, and brand styling.
acceptanceCriteria: API route at /api/og/[type]/[slug] returns a 1200x630 PNG image with title and type badge
Images use brand fonts and colors consistent with the landing page design tokens
Response includes proper Cache-Control headers (immutable, 1 year max-age with revalidation)

title: Auto-generated sitemap via Next.js sitemap.ts
description: Implement a dynamic sitemap.ts that auto-generates a complete sitemap including all static pages, HLD/LLD problem pages, concept pages, pattern pages, and blog posts with proper lastmod, changefreq, and priority values.
acceptanceCriteria: sitemap.ts generates entries for all page types with correct URLs and lastModified dates
Priority values are set appropriately (1.0 for landing, 0.8 for problems, 0.6 for concepts)
Sitemap output validates against the Sitemaps XML schema specification

title: Blog infrastructure (MDX, /blog route, RSS feed)
description: Set up blog infrastructure with MDX content support, a /blog index page with post listings, individual post pages at /blog/[slug], and an auto-generated RSS feed at /feed.xml.
acceptanceCriteria: MDX content pipeline configured with frontmatter parsing (title, date, excerpt, tags, author)
/blog page lists all posts sorted by date with title, excerpt, date, and reading time
RSS feed at /feed.xml auto-generates from all published blog posts with full content

title: First 5 blog posts with embedded interactive visualizations
description: Write 5 initial blog posts covering key system design topics. Each post includes embedded interactive React visualizations inline with the MDX content.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 blog posts written covering distinct topics (e.g., consistent hashing, load balancing, CAP theorem)
Each post includes at least one embedded interactive visualization component within the MDX
Posts are SEO-optimized with meta descriptions, OG images, and internal links to related problem pages

title: Newsletter signup with Resend integration
description: Build a newsletter signup system branded as 'The Architect's Digest'. Includes a signup form component, Resend API integration for email delivery, double opt-in confirmation, and unsubscribe handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Signup form validates email, calls API route, and shows success/error state with optimistic UI
API route creates contact in Resend audience and sends double opt-in confirmation email
Unsubscribe link in emails calls a handler that removes the contact from the audience

title: 15 company interview pages (/interviews/[company])
description: Create SEO-optimized interview guide pages for 15 major tech companies (Google, Meta, Amazon, Apple, Netflix, Microsoft, Uber, Airbnb, Stripe, Twitter, LinkedIn, Spotify, Dropbox, Pinterest, Slack).
acceptanceCriteria: generateStaticParams returns slugs for all 15 company pages
Each page includes company-specific interview format, common questions, prep tips, and related problems
Pages link to relevant HLD/LLD problems that the company frequently asks

title: Internal linking strategy implementation
description: Implement systematic internal linking across all SEO pages. Related problems link to each other, concepts link to problems that use them, and patterns link to related concepts and problems.
acceptanceCriteria: Each problem page includes a 'Related Problems' section with 3-5 links to similar problems
Each concept page links to problems and patterns that use the concept
A RelatedLinks component renders consistently across all page types with proper anchor text

title: robots.txt configuration
description: Configure robots.txt to allow search engine crawling of all public SEO pages while disallowing private routes (dashboard, API endpoints, auth). Include sitemap URL reference.
acceptanceCriteria: robots.txt allows crawling of all public pages (/, /problems/*, /concepts/*, /patterns/*, /blog/*)
Disallow rules block /dashboard/*, /api/*, /auth/* from crawlers
Sitemap URL is referenced in robots.txt pointing to the auto-generated sitemap

title: Canonical URLs for all pages
description: Ensure every page in the application has a canonical URL set to prevent duplicate content issues. Implement a utility that generates canonical URLs based on the page type and slug.
acceptanceCriteria: Every page includes a <link rel='canonical'> tag with the correct absolute URL
Utility function generates canonical URLs consistently using the base domain and page path
No trailing slashes or query parameters appear in canonical URLs

title: Meta description templates per page type
description: Create meta description template functions for each page type (problem, concept, pattern, blog, interview) that generate unique, keyword-rich descriptions under 160 characters.
acceptanceCriteria: Template functions exist for each page type that generate descriptions from page data
All generated descriptions are between 120-160 characters and include primary keywords
No two pages produce the same meta description

title: Twitter Card and Open Graph tags
description: Ensure all pages have proper Twitter Card (summary_large_image) and Open Graph tags set via Next.js metadata. Includes title, description, image, site name, and type.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages include og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type, and og:site_name
Twitter card meta tags set to summary_large_image with twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image
OG image URLs point to dynamically generated images from SEO-008

title: Product Hunt launch preparation
description: Prepare all assets and copy for a Product Hunt launch: product description, tagline, thumbnail (240x240), gallery images (1270x760), maker intro video script, and first comment draft.
acceptanceCriteria: Product Hunt description (max 260 chars), tagline, and 5 feature bullet points written
Thumbnail (240x240) and 5 gallery screenshots (1270x760) prepared as export-ready assets
First comment draft and maker story written and reviewed

title: Hacker News Show HN post preparation
description: Prepare a Hacker News 'Show HN' post including a compelling title, post body with technical details, and a FAQ for anticipated questions. Focus on technical depth and unique differentiators.
acceptanceCriteria: Show HN title written under 80 characters emphasizing the technical angle
Post body drafted highlighting the technical architecture, open-source aspects, and unique features
FAQ document prepared with responses to anticipated HN community questions

title: Yjs document structure for collaborative editing
description: Define the Yjs shared document structure using Y.Map for nodes, Y.Map for edges, and Y.Map for metadata (room name, owner, participants). Implement type-safe wrappers for CRUD operations on the shared types.
acceptanceCriteria: Y.Doc initialized with typed Y.Map structures for nodes, edges, and room metadata
Type-safe wrapper functions for add/update/delete operations on nodes and edges within Y.Doc
Unit tests verify concurrent modifications merge correctly without data loss

title: Zustand to Yjs bidirectional sync middleware
description: Build Zustand middleware that bidirectionally syncs the canvas store (nodes, edges) with the Yjs shared document. Local Zustand mutations propagate to Yjs, and remote Yjs updates propagate to Zustand.
acceptanceCriteria: Zustand middleware intercepts set calls and applies changes to the Y.Doc
Y.Doc observe callbacks update Zustand state for remote changes without triggering re-sync loops
Sync works correctly for add, update, delete, and bulk operations on nodes and edges

title: PartyKit server for WebSocket relay with JWT auth
description: Set up a PartyKit server as the WebSocket relay for Yjs document sync. Implement JWT-based authentication on connection, rate limiting per client, and room-scoped document persistence.
acceptanceCriteria: PartyKit server relays Yjs sync messages between connected clients in the same room
JWT token validated on WebSocket connection; invalid tokens rejected with 401
Rate limiting enforced at 100 messages/second per client with graceful backoff

title: Live cursors on canvas
description: Display live cursors for all connected collaborators on the canvas. Each cursor shows the user's pointer position with a colored arrow and name label. Positions are synced via Yjs awareness protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: Remote cursor positions render as colored arrows with name labels on the canvas
Cursor positions update in real-time via Yjs awareness with throttled updates (50ms)
Cursors fade out and disappear after 10 seconds of inactivity from a remote user

title: Selection rings for remote-selected nodes
description: Show colored dashed rings around nodes that are currently selected by remote collaborators. Each collaborator's selection is shown in their assigned color with their name tooltip.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes selected by remote users display a colored dashed ring matching the user's cursor color
Hovering over a selection ring shows a tooltip with the collaborator's name
Selection state syncs via Yjs awareness and clears when the remote user deselects

title: Follow mode (sync viewport with collaborator)
description: Implement a follow mode where clicking on a collaborator's avatar in the presence bar syncs your canvas viewport to match theirs. The viewport follows their pan and zoom in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a collaborator avatar activates follow mode with a visual indicator in the status bar
Local viewport smoothly animates to match the followed user's pan position and zoom level
Follow mode automatically disengages when the local user manually pans or zooms

title: Presence bar with avatars and status
description: Build a presence bar component showing avatars of all connected collaborators. Each avatar shows active/idle status with a colored ring, and hover shows the user's name and activity status.
acceptanceCriteria: Presence bar renders horizontally with avatar circles for each connected user
Active users have a green ring; idle users (no activity for 60s) have a yellow ring
Hover tooltip shows user name and current activity (e.g., 'Editing node: Load Balancer')

title: Shareable links (lz-string + AES-GCM encryption)
description: Implement shareable links for designs. Small designs use lz-string compression in the URL hash. Large designs use AES-GCM encryption with a server-stored payload and a decryption key in the URL fragment.
acceptanceCriteria: Designs under 2KB serialize to lz-string compressed URL hash that decodes on page load
Designs over 2KB encrypt payload with AES-GCM, store on server, and put decryption key in URL fragment
Share dialog shows a copyable link with a 'Copy' button and success confirmation

title: Community Gallery page (/gallery)
description: Build a community gallery page at /gallery that displays publicly shared designs. Supports sorting by trending, newest, and most liked. Includes pagination and preview cards with screenshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Gallery page renders a grid of design preview cards with title, author, like count, and thumbnail
Sort tabs for Trending, Newest, and Most Liked filter the results correctly
Infinite scroll or pagination loads additional results on demand

title: Upvote system with optimistic UI
description: Implement an upvote/like system for gallery designs. Toggle-based (click to like, click again to unlike) with optimistic UI updates and server-side deduplication per user.
acceptanceCriteria: Like button toggles state with immediate optimistic count update before server confirmation
Server endpoint handles like/unlike with user deduplication (one like per user per design)
If server request fails, optimistic update is reverted with an error toast

title: Threaded comment system with markdown support
description: Build a comment system for gallery designs with one level of threaded replies. Comments support Markdown rendering and are moderation-ready with report functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can post top-level comments and one-level reply threads on gallery designs
Comment content renders Markdown (bold, italic, code, links) with XSS sanitization
Each comment has a timestamp, author avatar, and a report button for moderation

title: Fork/remix with attribution
description: Allow users to fork (deep copy) a shared design into their own workspace. The forked design includes attribution linking back to the original design and author.
acceptanceCriteria: Fork button on gallery designs creates a deep copy in the user's workspace with a new ID
Forked design metadata includes original design ID, original author, and 'Forked from' label
Fork count is displayed on the original design's gallery card

title: Public user profile page (/profile/[username])
description: Create a public user profile page showing the user's published designs, fork count, total likes received, and a bio. Accessible at /profile/[username].
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page displays user avatar, name, bio, join date, and aggregate stats (designs, likes, forks)
Published designs grid shows the user's public designs with the same card format as gallery
Profile page has proper SEO metadata and OG tags for social sharing

title: OG image generation for shared designs
description: Generate Open Graph preview images for shared designs showing a canvas thumbnail with the design title and author. Used when sharing design links on social media and messaging platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: OG image route generates a 1200x630 image with a canvas thumbnail, title, and author name
Shared design pages reference the generated OG image in their meta tags
Images are cached with appropriate Cache-Control headers for performance

title: oEmbed provider for Slack/Notion auto-expand
description: Implement an oEmbed provider endpoint so that shared design URLs auto-expand with a rich preview in Slack, Notion, and other oEmbed-consuming platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: oEmbed discovery link tag present in shared design page HTML head
oEmbed JSON endpoint returns type 'rich' with title, author, thumbnail, and iframe HTML
Preview renders correctly when pasting a shared design URL in Slack and Notion

title: Toast notification system (ToastProvider + useToast)
description: Build a toast notification system with a ToastProvider context, useToast hook, and 4 variants: success, error, warning, and info. Toasts auto-dismiss after a configurable duration and stack vertically.
acceptanceCriteria: useToast hook exposes toast() function accepting message, variant, and optional duration
Toasts render in a fixed position (bottom-right) with enter/exit animations and stack up to 5
Each variant (success, error, warning, info) has a distinct color and icon

title: ConfirmDialog component
description: Create a reusable confirmation dialog component with customizable title, message, confirm/cancel button labels, and keyboard support (Enter to confirm, Escape to cancel).
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmDialog accepts title, message, confirmLabel, cancelLabel, and onConfirm/onCancel callbacks
Dialog traps focus, supports Enter to confirm and Escape to cancel
Renders with a backdrop overlay and smooth open/close animation

title: Error boundary wrappers on each module slot
description: Wrap each major module slot (canvas, sidebar panels, command palette, toolbar) with React error boundaries that catch rendering errors and show a friendly fallback with a retry button.
acceptanceCriteria: Error boundaries wrap canvas, left panel, right panel, toolbar, and command palette independently
Fallback UI shows a friendly error message with a 'Retry' button that resets the error boundary
Errors are logged to the error reporting service (Sentry) with component stack traces

title: Canvas empty state card
description: Show a helpful empty state card in the center of the canvas when no nodes exist. The card suggests actions: 'Drag components from the palette' or 'Load a template'.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty state card appears centered on canvas when node count is zero
Card includes an illustration/icon, descriptive text, and two action buttons (palette, templates)
Card disappears immediately when the first node is added to the canvas

title: Node hover effect (scale, border, tooltip)
description: Add hover effects to canvas nodes: subtle scale up (1.02), brighter border color, and a tooltip showing the node type and label on hover after a short delay.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes scale to 1.02 with a brighter border on mouse hover with smooth CSS transition
Tooltip appears after 500ms hover delay showing node type and label, disappears on mouse leave
Hover effects do not trigger during canvas pan/drag operations

title: Edge hover highlight and type label
description: When hovering over a canvas edge, highlight it with increased opacity/width and show a floating label indicating the edge type (e.g., 'REST API', 'gRPC', 'Pub/Sub').
acceptanceCriteria: Edge stroke width increases and color brightens on hover with smooth transition
Floating label appears near the cursor showing the edge's connection type
Hover detection works on the edge path with a reasonable hit area (8px tolerance)

title: Panel open/close spring animations
description: Add spring-based open/close animations to all sidebar panels (left and right). Panels slide in from their respective edges with a natural spring curve.
acceptanceCriteria: Left panel slides in from the left edge with a spring animation (no bounce overshoot)
Right panel slides in from the right edge with matching spring parameters
Panels animate to closed state (slide out) with the same spring curve on close

title: Module switch crossfade transition
description: Add a crossfade transition when switching between modules (System Design, Algorithm, Interview). The outgoing module fades out while the incoming module fades in.
acceptanceCriteria: Module content crossfades with 200ms duration when switching between modules
No layout shift occurs during the transition (outgoing and incoming overlap during crossfade)
Transition is skippable (rapid switching immediately shows the final module)

title: Drag ghost preview for palette items
description: When dragging a component from the palette onto the canvas, show a semi-transparent ghost preview of the node that follows the cursor during the drag operation.
acceptanceCriteria: Drag ghost renders a semi-transparent (50% opacity) preview of the node matching its actual appearance
Ghost follows the cursor smoothly during the drag with no jitter
Ghost snaps to grid when within the canvas drop zone if grid snapping is enabled

title: Drop zone highlighting on canvas during drag
description: When dragging a component from the palette, highlight the canvas drop zone with a pulsing border and a subtle background tint to indicate where the component can be dropped.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas area shows a pulsing border highlight when a palette item is being dragged over it
Drop zone background shows a subtle brand-color tint during dragover
Highlight disappears immediately on drop or drag cancel

title: Node creation pulse animation
description: When a new node is added to the canvas (via drag-drop or programmatic creation), play a brief pulse animation: the node scales up slightly and emits a radial glow before settling to its normal state.
acceptanceCriteria: Newly created nodes play a 400ms pulse animation (scale 1.0 -> 1.05 -> 1.0 with glow)
Animation uses CSS keyframes and does not block interaction with the node
Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion by skipping when set to reduce

title: Button click feedback (active state scale)
description: Add tactile click feedback to all interactive buttons: a brief scale-down on mouse/touch press (active state) that returns to normal on release.
acceptanceCriteria: All Button component variants include an active state with transform scale(0.97)
Scale transition uses a fast 50ms duration for snappy feedback feel
Active state applies on both mouse click and touch press events

title: Command palette open/close animation
description: Add a smooth open/close animation to the command palette: fade in the backdrop, slide the palette down from the top with a slight scale, and reverse on close.
acceptanceCriteria: Command palette fades in backdrop (0 -> 50% opacity) and slides palette from top with 150ms duration
Close animation reverses the open animation smoothly without jarring cuts
Animation does not delay keyboard input readiness (input is auto-focused during animation)

title: Delete confirmation for nodes with connections
description: When deleting a node that has active connections (edges), show a confirmation dialog warning the user that connected edges will also be removed. List the affected connections.
acceptanceCriteria: Deleting a node with 1+ edges triggers a ConfirmDialog listing the edges that will be removed
Dialog shows count and names of affected connections (e.g., 'This will remove 3 connections')
Nodes with no connections delete immediately without the confirmation dialog

title: Clear canvas confirmation dialog
description: When the user triggers 'Clear Canvas' (toolbar button or keyboard shortcut), show a confirmation dialog warning that all nodes and edges will be removed. This action should be undoable.
acceptanceCriteria: Clear Canvas action triggers a ConfirmDialog with warning message and destructive-styled confirm button
Confirming clears all nodes and edges from the canvas and pushes the action onto the undo stack
Cancel returns to the canvas with no changes

title: Discard unsaved changes beforeunload handler
description: Implement a beforeunload handler that warns users when they attempt to close the tab or navigate away with unsaved changes. Track dirty state by comparing current canvas state against last saved state.
acceptanceCriteria: Browser shows native 'Leave site? Changes you made may not be saved' dialog on tab close when dirty
Dirty state tracks correctly: set on any canvas change, cleared on save or auto-save
Handler does not trigger when canvas state matches the last saved state

title: Auto-save every 30s to IndexedDB
description: Implement auto-save that persists the current canvas state to IndexedDB every 30 seconds when there are unsaved changes. Uses a debounced interval to avoid excessive writes.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas state auto-saves to IndexedDB every 30 seconds when the dirty flag is set
Auto-save uses a structured IndexedDB store with design ID as key and serialized state as value
On app load, auto-saved state is restored if it is newer than the last server-saved version

title: Save indicator in status bar
description: Show a save status indicator in the bottom status bar with three states: 'Saved' (green check), 'Saving...' (spinner), and 'Unsaved changes' (yellow dot). Updates reactively based on save state.
acceptanceCriteria: Status bar shows 'Saved' with green check icon when no unsaved changes exist
Status changes to 'Saving...' with a spinner during auto-save or manual save operations
Status shows 'Unsaved changes' with a yellow dot when dirty flag is set and no save is in progress

title: Skeleton loading states for all panels
description: Create skeleton loading components for each panel type (palette, properties, simulation, chat). Show animated placeholder shapes matching the panel layout while data loads.
acceptanceCriteria: Skeleton components exist for palette, properties, simulation results, and chat panels
Skeletons match the approximate layout of their loaded counterparts (rectangles for text, circles for icons)
Skeletons use a shimmer animation with the same timing across all panel types

title: First-time user detection via localStorage
description: Implement first-time user detection using a localStorage flag. On first visit, set the flag and trigger the onboarding tour. Provide a useIsFirstVisit hook for components to check.
acceptanceCriteria: useIsFirstVisit hook returns true on first visit and false on subsequent visits
localStorage key 'architex-visited' is set after the first visit is acknowledged
Hook includes a reset function for testing and a 'Show tour again' settings option

title: Tooltip tour / guided walkthrough (5 steps)
description: Build a 5-step guided walkthrough tour that highlights key UI areas for first-time users: 1) Canvas, 2) Component Palette, 3) Properties Panel, 4) Simulation Button, 5) Command Palette shortcut.
acceptanceCriteria: Tour highlights 5 UI elements sequentially with a tooltip overlay pointing to each element
Each step has a title, description, and Next/Skip buttons with keyboard support
Tour progress is tracked and can be resumed if the user navigates away mid-tour

title: Keyboard shortcuts dialog (? trigger)
description: Create a keyboard shortcuts reference dialog that opens when the user presses '?'. Shows a categorized list of all available shortcuts with their key combinations.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing '?' (when not in an input field) opens the keyboard shortcuts dialog
Shortcuts are grouped by category (Canvas, Nodes, Navigation, General) with key combination badges
Dialog is searchable/filterable and closes on Escape or clicking outside

title: Settings page (theme, notifications, subscription, account)
description: Build a settings page with tabbed sections for: Theme (light/dark/system), Notifications (email preferences), Subscription (current plan, upgrade), and Account (profile, email, delete).
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page has 4 tabbed sections with proper URL routing (/settings/theme, /settings/account, etc.)
Theme section allows switching between light, dark, and system modes with preview
Account section includes profile editing, email change, and account deletion with confirmation

title: Recently used commands in command palette
description: Track recently used commands in the command palette and display them in a 'Recent' section at the top of the results list when the search input is empty.
acceptanceCriteria: Last 5 used commands are stored in localStorage and shown in a 'Recent' section when input is empty
Recent commands are ordered by last-used timestamp (most recent first)
Selecting a recent command executes it and moves it to the top of the recent list

title: Contextual tooltips on first encounter with features
description: Show contextual tooltip hints when users encounter features for the first time (e.g., first time opening properties panel, first simulation). Track seen features in localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: Contextual tooltips appear once per feature on first encounter with a dismiss button
Seen tooltips are tracked in localStorage so they do not reappear
At least 8 contextual tooltips defined for key features (properties, simulation, export, etc.)

title: README.md rewrite with project overview and architecture
description: Rewrite the project README.md with: project overview, hero screenshot, badges, quick start guide, architecture diagram (Mermaid), feature highlights, tech stack, and contribution links.
acceptanceCriteria: README includes project description, hero screenshot/GIF, and technology badges at the top
Quick start section provides 4-step setup instructions (clone, install, env, run)
Architecture overview section includes a Mermaid diagram showing the system components

title: CONTRIBUTING.md with setup, branching, and PR process
description: Write a CONTRIBUTING.md covering: local development setup, branch naming conventions, commit message format, PR process, code review expectations, and code of conduct reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Document covers local setup prerequisites (Node.js version, pnpm, env vars) with copy-paste commands
Branch naming (feature/, fix/, chore/) and commit message conventions (conventional commits) documented
PR process documented including template reference, review SLA, and merge requirements

title: How to add a new module guide
description: Write a developer guide explaining how to add a new module to the application. Covers the module registry, route setup, sidebar entry, store slice, and component scaffold.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide walks through 5 steps from module definition to rendering in the app
Includes code snippets for each step with file paths and expected output
A checklist at the end verifies the module appears in navigation and loads correctly

title: How to add a new node type guide
description: Write a developer guide explaining how to add a new node type to the canvas. Covers the node type definition, renderer component, properties panel config, palette entry, and serialization.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide covers node type schema, custom renderer, properties panel fields, and palette registration
Includes a complete worked example (e.g., adding a 'CDN' node type) with all files listed
Testing section explains how to verify the node renders, serializes, and connects correctly

title: How to add a new algorithm guide
description: Write a developer guide explaining how to add a new algorithm visualization. Covers algorithm definition, step generation, visualization component, and integration with the algorithm module.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide covers algorithm metadata, step generator function, and visualization component implementation
Includes a complete worked example with a simple algorithm (e.g., binary search)
Explains how to add algorithm-specific controls and customize the visualization

title: How to add a new template guide
description: Write a developer guide explaining how to add a new design template. Covers template definition (nodes, edges, metadata), thumbnail generation, and registration in the template gallery.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide covers template JSON structure, metadata fields, and gallery registration
Includes a complete worked example of creating a template from an existing design
Explains thumbnail generation process and preview rendering in the template picker

title: 8 Architecture Decision Records (ADR-001 through ADR-008)
description: Write 8 ADRs covering major architectural decisions: state management (Zustand), canvas rendering (React Flow), real-time sync (Yjs/PartyKit), auth (Clerk), database (Drizzle/Neon), styling (Tailwind), deployment (Vercel), and AI integration (Claude).
acceptanceCriteria: 8 ADR files follow the standard format: Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences
Each ADR explains what alternatives were considered and why the chosen option was selected
ADRs are indexed in an ADR README with links and one-line summaries

title: Bug report issue template
description: Create a GitHub issue template for bug reports with structured fields: description, steps to reproduce, expected behavior, actual behavior, environment info, and screenshots.
acceptanceCriteria: YAML issue template at .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml with all required fields
Template includes dropdown for severity (critical, major, minor, cosmetic)
Environment section collects browser, OS, and app version information

title: Feature request issue template
description: Create a GitHub issue template for feature requests with structured fields: problem statement, proposed solution, alternatives considered, and additional context.
acceptanceCriteria: YAML issue template at .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.yml with structured fields
Template includes a 'Problem statement' field describing the user pain point
Template includes 'Proposed solution' and 'Alternatives considered' text areas

title: Pull request template
description: Create a GitHub pull request template with sections: summary of changes, type of change (feature/fix/refactor), related issues, testing checklist, and screenshots.
acceptanceCriteria: PR template at .github/pull_request_template.md with all sections populated with placeholder text
Template includes a checkbox checklist for testing, documentation, and review readiness
Type of change section uses checkboxes for feature, bug fix, refactor, docs, and breaking change

title: LICENSE file (AGPL-3.0)
description: Add the AGPL-3.0 license file to the repository root. Include the standard AGPL-3.0 text with the correct copyright holder and year.
acceptanceCriteria: LICENSE file contains the full AGPL-3.0 license text
Copyright line includes the correct year and copyright holder name
package.json license field updated to 'AGPL-3.0-only'

title: LICENSE-CONTENT file (CC BY-SA 4.0)
description: Add a separate LICENSE-CONTENT file for educational content (problems, concepts, tutorials) under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0. Clearly scope which content it covers.
acceptanceCriteria: LICENSE-CONTENT file contains the CC BY-SA 4.0 license text
File header clearly states which directories/files fall under this license
README references both licenses with a brief explanation of the dual-license structure

title: Terms of Service draft
description: Draft a Terms of Service document covering: service description, user accounts, acceptable use, intellectual property, limitation of liability, termination, and governing law.
acceptanceCriteria: ToS covers 8+ standard sections including acceptable use, IP, liability, and termination
Document includes placeholders for company legal name, jurisdiction, and effective date
Accessible at /terms route with proper metadata

title: Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy draft
description: Draft Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy documents covering: data collection, usage, storage, third-party sharing, cookies, analytics, user rights (GDPR/CCPA), and contact information.
acceptanceCriteria: Privacy Policy covers data collection, processing purposes, retention, and user rights (GDPR + CCPA)
Cookie Policy lists all cookies by category (essential, analytics, preferences) with purposes
Both documents accessible at /privacy and /cookies routes with proper metadata

title: .env.example with all required variables
description: Create a .env.example file listing all required and optional environment variables with descriptions, default values where applicable, and links to where to obtain API keys.
acceptanceCriteria: .env.example lists all environment variables grouped by service (DB, Auth, AI, Analytics, etc.)
Each variable has a comment describing its purpose and where to obtain the value
Required vs optional variables are clearly marked with (required) and (optional) labels

title: Stripe SDK setup and configuration
description: Install and configure the Stripe SDK (stripe and @stripe/stripe-js). Set up server-side and client-side Stripe instances with proper environment variable handling.
acceptanceCriteria: stripe (server) and @stripe/stripe-js (client) packages installed and configured
Server-side Stripe instance initialized with STRIPE_SECRET_KEY from environment variables
Client-side loadStripe initialized with NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY

title: Stripe Checkout session API route
description: Create an API route that creates a Stripe Checkout session for plan upgrades. Handles plan selection, customer creation/lookup, and redirects to Stripe's hosted checkout page.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/billing/checkout creates a Checkout session with the selected price ID and returns the URL
Existing Stripe customer is looked up by user ID; new customer created if not found
Success and cancel URLs are configured to redirect back to the appropriate app pages

title: Stripe webhook handler for subscription lifecycle
description: Implement a Stripe webhook handler that processes subscription lifecycle events: checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted, and invoice.payment_failed.
acceptanceCriteria: Webhook endpoint at /api/billing/webhook validates Stripe signature and processes 4+ event types
Subscription status changes are reflected in the database user record within the webhook handler
Failed payment events trigger a notification to the user and set account to grace period

title: Usage-based limits (5 free simulations/month)
description: Implement usage tracking and enforcement for free-tier users. Track simulation count per billing period and block simulations after 5/month with an upgrade prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulation count tracked per user per calendar month in the database
Free-tier users see remaining simulation count in the UI and get blocked after 5 with an upgrade CTA
Pro/Team tier users have unlimited simulations with no counter displayed

title: Stripe Customer Portal redirect
description: Create an API route that generates a Stripe Customer Portal session URL for users to manage their subscription, update payment methods, and view invoices.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/billing/portal returns a Stripe Customer Portal session URL
Portal session is configured with the return URL pointing back to the settings page
Users without an active subscription are redirected to the pricing page instead

title: Pricing page UI (3 tiers, feature comparison)
description: Build a standalone pricing page at /pricing with 3 plan tiers (Free, Pro, Team), a detailed feature comparison table, and checkout CTAs that initiate the Stripe Checkout flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Pricing page renders 3 tier cards with plan name, price, feature list, and CTA button
Feature comparison table below the cards compares all features across the 3 tiers
CTA buttons call the checkout API route and redirect to Stripe Checkout

title: 7-day free trial flow
description: Implement a 7-day free trial for the Pro plan. New users can start a trial without entering payment info. Trial status is tracked and users are prompted to convert before expiration.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can start a 7-day Pro trial from the pricing page without entering payment info
Trial end date is stored and a countdown banner shows remaining trial days in the app
Users receive an email reminder 2 days before trial expiration with a conversion CTA

title: Annual/monthly toggle with 20% savings display
description: Add a billing period toggle (monthly/annual) to the pricing page and pricing section. Annual plans show a '20% savings' badge and the per-month equivalent price.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle switch switches between monthly and annual pricing with smooth price transition animation
Annual pricing cards display a 'Save 20%' badge and show the monthly equivalent (e.g., '$16/mo billed annually')
Selected billing period is passed to the Stripe Checkout session as the correct price ID

title: Shared workspaces with team-scoped rooms and RBAC
description: Implement shared workspaces where teams can collaborate on designs. Each workspace has team-scoped rooms with role-based access control (owner, admin, member, viewer).
acceptanceCriteria: Workspace CRUD operations with team membership management (invite, remove, role change)
RBAC enforced at API level: owners can delete workspace, admins manage members, members edit, viewers read-only
Workspace switcher in the app header allows switching between personal and team workspaces

title: Team progress dashboard
description: Build a team progress dashboard showing aggregated learning metrics: problems completed, concepts mastered, simulation scores, and individual member progress breakdowns.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays team-level aggregate metrics (total problems, avg score, completion rate)
Individual member rows show per-person progress with a progress bar and key stats
Dashboard data refreshes on page load and can be filtered by time period (week, month, all)

title: Mock interview pairing
description: Implement a mock interview pairing system where team members can be matched for practice interviews. Includes scheduling, role assignment (interviewer/candidate), and feedback forms.
acceptanceCriteria: Pairing system matches two team members with configurable time slots and problem selection
Each session assigns interviewer and candidate roles with a shared collaborative room
Post-session feedback form collects structured ratings and comments from both participants

title: Custom learning paths (admin creates, assigns)
description: Allow workspace admins to create custom learning paths: ordered sequences of problems, concepts, and simulations that can be assigned to team members with deadlines.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin UI for creating learning paths by selecting and ordering content items
Learning paths can be assigned to individual members or the entire team with optional deadlines
Assigned members see the learning path in their dashboard with progress tracking

title: SSO via SAML/OIDC using Clerk
description: Enable enterprise SSO through Clerk's SAML and OIDC support. Allow workspace admins to configure SSO with their identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace).
acceptanceCriteria: Clerk SAML/OIDC enterprise connection configured and accessible from workspace admin settings
SSO login flow redirects to the identity provider and creates/links user accounts on return
Admin can enforce SSO-only login for their workspace (disable email/password for team members)

title: Custom content upload for enterprise
description: Allow enterprise workspace admins to upload custom content (problems, templates, learning materials) that is scoped to their workspace and available only to their team.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin upload UI accepts custom problems (title, description, requirements) in structured format
Custom content is workspace-scoped and only visible to workspace members
Uploaded content appears alongside platform content with a 'Custom' badge in the UI

title: Skills assessment with auto-calculated readiness
description: Build an automated skills assessment system that calculates interview readiness scores based on problem completion, simulation performance, concept mastery, and practice frequency.
acceptanceCriteria: Readiness score calculated as a weighted composite of 4 dimensions (problems, simulations, concepts, practice)
Individual dimension scores shown on a radar chart with the overall readiness percentage
Assessment updates automatically as the user completes activities (no manual trigger needed)

title: LMS integration (SCORM/LTI)
description: Implement LMS integration support via SCORM and LTI standards. Allow enterprise customers to embed Architex content into their existing learning management systems.
acceptanceCriteria: LTI 1.3 provider endpoint configured for tool launches from external LMS platforms
SCORM 2004 package export available for offline/embedded content delivery
Progress and completion data transmitted back to the LMS via the appropriate standard

title: requireAuth() on every server boundary
description: Audit and enforce authentication on every server-side boundary: API routes, server actions, and server components accessing user data. Create a requireAuth() utility that throws on unauthenticated access.
acceptanceCriteria: requireAuth() utility wraps Clerk's auth() and throws a 401 error if no valid session exists
Every API route and server action that accesses user data uses requireAuth() as its first call
CI lint rule or ESLint plugin flags API route handlers that do not call requireAuth()

title: XSS sanitization with DOMPurify and Zod
description: Implement XSS protection on all user inputs: server-side Zod validation with string sanitization, and client-side DOMPurify for any rendered user content (comments, bios, custom content).
acceptanceCriteria: All API route input schemas use Zod with .transform() to strip HTML tags and sanitize strings
DOMPurify applied to all user-generated content before rendering (comments, bios, descriptions)
XSS test suite with common attack vectors (script injection, event handlers, SVG payloads) all pass

title: API key protection with CI verification
description: Verify no server-side secrets are exposed via NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefixed variables. Add a CI grep check that fails the build if any secret-pattern variables have the NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit confirms no STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URL, or other secrets use NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix
CI pipeline step greps .env* files and source code for NEXT_PUBLIC_ with secret-pattern names
Build fails with a clear error message if a secret is found with NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix

title: Sentry beforeSend PII scrubbing
description: Configure Sentry's beforeSend hook to scrub personally identifiable information (PII) from error reports: email addresses, IP addresses, auth tokens, and user-generated content.
acceptanceCriteria: beforeSend hook strips email addresses and auth tokens from event data and breadcrumbs
User context sends only a hashed user ID, never email or name
IP address collection is disabled in Sentry project settings

title: Content Security Policy headers (nonce-based script-src)
description: Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers using nonce-based script-src to prevent inline script injection. Configure CSP for all page types including the landing page and app.
acceptanceCriteria: CSP header set with nonce-based script-src, restricting inline scripts to those with valid nonces
Next.js middleware generates a unique nonce per request and passes it to the CSP header and Script tags
CSP violations are reported to a /api/csp-report endpoint for monitoring

title: HSTS + X-Content-Type-Options + X-Frame-Options headers
description: Configure security response headers: HSTS with max-age and includeSubDomains, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, and X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent common web attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: HSTS header set with max-age=31536000, includeSubDomains, and preload on all responses
X-Content-Type-Options set to nosniff to prevent MIME type sniffing
X-Frame-Options set to DENY to prevent clickjacking (with exceptions for oEmbed iframes)

title: CORS allowlist configuration
description: Configure CORS headers with an explicit allowlist of permitted origins. Only the production domain, preview deployments, and localhost during development should be allowed.
acceptanceCriteria: CORS middleware checks Origin header against an allowlist defined in environment variables
Only production domain, *.vercel.app preview URLs, and localhost:3000 are permitted origins
Requests from non-allowed origins receive no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header

title: Rate limiting per endpoint (Arcjet/Upstash)
description: Implement rate limiting on all API endpoints using Arcjet or Upstash Redis. Configure different limits per endpoint type: auth endpoints (stricter), read endpoints, and write endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Rate limiter middleware applied to all API routes with configurable limits per route pattern
Auth endpoints limited to 10 req/min, write endpoints to 30 req/min, read endpoints to 100 req/min
Rate-limited responses return 429 with Retry-After header and a human-readable error message

title: Decompression bomb prevention on shared URLs
description: Protect the shared URL decoder from decompression bomb attacks. Limit the decompressed payload size and add timeout protection on the decompression operation.
acceptanceCriteria: Decompression of shared URL payloads is limited to 1MB maximum decompressed size
Decompression operation times out after 500ms to prevent CPU exhaustion
Payloads exceeding limits return a 413 error with a clear message about size limits

title: GDPR data export endpoint
description: Create a GDPR-compliant data export endpoint that allows users to download all their personal data as a structured JSON file: profile, designs, comments, likes, and usage data.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/account/export returns a JSON file containing all user data categories
Export includes profile, designs, comments, likes, subscriptions, and activity history
Export is rate-limited to 1 request per 24 hours and generates a download link valid for 1 hour

title: GDPR account deletion with grace period
description: Implement account deletion with a 30-day grace period. User initiates deletion, account is soft-deleted and data anonymized after 30 days. Cancellation is possible during the grace period.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /api/account initiates soft deletion with a 30-day grace period
User receives confirmation email with a link to cancel the deletion during the grace period
After 30 days, a scheduled job permanently deletes and anonymizes all user data

title: Cookie consent banner with conditional PostHog loading
description: Implement a cookie consent banner that blocks PostHog analytics loading until the user explicitly consents. Supports accept/reject/manage options with preference persistence.
acceptanceCriteria: Cookie consent banner appears on first visit with Accept, Reject, and Manage Preferences options
PostHog script only loads after the user explicitly accepts analytics cookies
User preferences are stored in a cookie and respected on subsequent visits without re-prompting
